H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble (n=95) presents mean 7.15 (std 1.78) with strong confidence, the largest‑n protocol. Distribution is right‑leaning with a peak at 8. Qualitative responses (n=29) emphasize noticeable improvement and a 10‑minute settle; music receives many loved votes. YouTube reach is highest (30 views). AutoGluon predicts 6.86, underestimating observed performance, but cross‑buckets to X‑ME‑L1/L2 suggest mood‑lifting potential.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Great for overstimulation; people feel calmer and many love the music, though most note only noticeable change.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall (n=51) shows mean 6.92 (std 1.73) with strong confidence; distribution peaks at 7‑8. Qualitative feedback (n=21) highlights very settling rhythm and mixed music opinions. YouTube views: 5. AutoGluon predicts 6.86 for overstim, close to observed.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Helps calm overstimulated listeners with a very settling rhythm, though music reception is mixed.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier (n=47) yields mean 7.04 (std 1.68) with meaningful confidence; distribution is smooth with peak at 8. Qualitative data (n=23) reveals a notable caregiver usage and many listeners trying to sleep, indicating cross‑zone repurposing. YouTube views: 8. AutoGluon cross‑bucket does not highlight this protocol despite its distinct semantic embedding (lowest mean similarity).
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Works well for overstimulation and even helps some fall asleep, with generally positive feedback.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Meadow (n=67) yields a mean 7.22 (std 1.93) with strong confidence. The right‑skewed distribution clusters at scores 8‑10, indicating pronounced relief. Qualitative data (n=23) reports moderate to noticeable improvement and settle times around 5‑10 min; 13 listeners were self‑reported. Adviser routing contributed 5 % of responses. AutoGluon predicts 7.84 for the anxiety zone, modestly above observed, and cross‑buckets X‑ME‑L1 and X‑MD‑L1, suggesting broader utility. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views, no retention).
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners report a strong calming effect after about 5‑10 minutes, and most rate the music highly.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage (n=48) shows mean 6.94 (std 1.62) with meaningful confidence and a unimodal peak at 7‑8. Qualitative responses (n=20) indicate a faster 5‑minute settle for many, though 4 note a 20‑minute settle, reflecting a bimodal engagement pattern. Music opinions are split between liked and neutral. No YouTube data available. AutoGluon does not flag a cross‑bucket for this protocol.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Many notice improvement quickly, though some need a longer listen; overall music is liked.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley (n=36) reports mean 7.08 (std 1.89) with meaningful confidence; distribution is broader with tails toward high scores. Qualitative input (n=20) highlights a 10‑minute settle for most, but some listeners only feel effect near session end, suggesting varied entrainment timing. Rhythm is described as very settling by many. YouTube views are low (4). Drift similarity is 0.695, indicating moderate analytical shift.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Generally effective for anxiety with very soothing rhythms, though a few need the full session to feel it.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor (n=43) yields mean 6.35 (std 1.70) with meaningful confidence, matching expected lower scores for crisis entry. Distribution peaks at 8 with a long low‑score tail. Qualitative data (n=24) shows 8 participants reporting significant improvement and rapid 5‑minute settle. Adviser routing absent. AutoGluon predicts a higher crisis score (7.96) for H‑CR‑L2, highlighting a model‑data gap. YouTube views: 6.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Even in acute distress, listeners often feel better fast, with many noting significant improvement.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor (n=33) records mean 6.52 (std 1.70) with meaningful confidence; scores concentrate around 7‑8. Qualitative feedback (n=16) is mixed on music (neutral vs. loved) and reports balanced improvement levels. AutoGluon assigns the highest crisis prediction (7.96) to this protocol, substantially above the observed mean, suggesting potential under‑realized efficacy. YouTube views: 4.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Crisis listeners report steady improvement; music opinion varies but outcomes are solid.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon (n=27) has mean 6.52 (std 1.91) with meaningful confidence; distribution is wide with an immediate settle mode (7 of 19 within 1‑2 min). Qualitative comments note 4 ‘Not liked’ music ratings, possibly driving variance. No adviser entries. AutoGluon does not list cross‑bucket routing for this protocol. YouTube views: 5.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Quickly helps some in crisis, though a few dislike the music, leading to mixed feelings.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern (n=54) records mean 7.19 (std 1.86) with strong confidence; distribution spans low to high scores, reflecting diverse sleep disturbance levels. Qualitative input (n=15) shows fast 5‑minute settle and strong music praise. YouTube views: 4. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, aligning well; drift similarity is 0.530, the most pronounced in HEALING.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Supports sleep onset quickly; listeners find the rhythm comforting and music enjoyable.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock (n=45) displays mean 7.09 (std 1.55) with meaningful confidence; distribution is tight around 7‑8. Qualitative responses (n=15) are dominated by caregiver and patient listeners, with some noting ‘Too busy’ rhythm, hinting at a population effect on variance. YouTube views: 5.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Provides steady sleep support, often used by caregivers; music is generally well received.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight (n=28) has mean 7.07 (std 1.68) with meaningful confidence; distribution is even across 6‑9. Qualitative feedback (n=22) reports balanced improvement levels and a fast 5‑minute settle, though rhythm ‘slightly busy’ appears in several comments. YouTube views: 3.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Helps many settle for sleep, though a few find the rhythm a bit busy.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (n=41) shows mean 6.46 (std 1.82) with meaningful confidence; distribution includes a notable low‑score mass and ‘slightly busy’ rhythm opinions (9 of 23). Qualitative data (n=23) reports noticeable improvement but rhythm busyness may depress scores. AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for focus, indicating a gap. YouTube views: 5.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus improves but some find the rhythm a little busy, which may hold back the effect.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (n=32) yields mean 7.19 (std 1.60) with meaningful confidence; distribution centers at 8. Qualitative input (n=17) notes moderate to significant improvement and balanced rhythm, though some find it slightly busy. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, close to observed. YouTube views: 4.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Better focus with moderate improvement; rhythm is still a bit busy for some listeners.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (n=35) records mean 7.63 (std 1.97) with meaningful confidence; distribution is bimodal with many 10 scores and a low‑score tail, suggesting population heterogeneity. Qualitative data (n=29) shows high significant‑improvement reports and a caregiver‑heavy listener mix. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, aligning with observed high efficacy. YouTube views: 15. Drift similarity 0.532.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Excellent focus support; many report significant improvement and highly praise the experience.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember (n=34) yields mean 6.74 (std 1.73) with meaningful confidence; distribution is flat from 5‑9. Qualitative data (n=21) shows slight improvement dominant, with a bimodal settle‑time (immediate vs. 10 min). No YouTube data. AutoGluon does not rank this protocol in top creative flow predictions.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Gentle boost to creative flow; many notice only slight improvement at first.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current (n=24) reports mean 6.79 (std 1.41) with meaningful confidence; distribution tight around 7‑8. Qualitative feedback (n=15) notes significant improvement and strong music love, indicating higher satisfaction than L1. No YouTube data.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: More noticeable creative lift; listeners love the music and feel stronger improvement.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (n=18) presents mean 7.28 (std 1.32) with developing confidence; distribution is upper‑leaning with several high scores. Qualitative responses (n=25) include many proxy listeners and a 10‑minute settle, suggesting cross‑zone utility. YouTube views: 7.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Strong creative boost; most listeners report noticeable improvement and like the rhythm.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone (n=25) has mean 7.04 (std 1.34) with meaningful confidence; distribution tight around 7‑8. Qualitative feedback (n=23) includes many therapy‑session contexts and balanced improvement ratings. AutoGluon predicts 7.69 for maintenance, slightly above observed. YouTube views: 3.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Consistent maintenance support, especially in therapy settings; listeners report moderate improvement.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis (n=22) yields mean 6.68 (std 2.03) with meaningful confidence; distribution is wide with rhythm opinions split between balanced and slightly busy. Qualitative data (n=16) shows mixed listener types. AutoGluon predicts 7.69, indicating potential under‑performance. YouTube views: 4.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed maintenance results; some listeners note busy rhythms while others improve modestly.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (n=14) presents mean 6.93 (std 1.73) with developing confidence; some listeners (4 of 19) did not settle, hinting at entrainment issues. Qualitative responses (n=19) are mixed but overall improvement is reported. AutoGluon predicts 7.69, suggesting extending hold duration could help. YouTube views: 3.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Generally good maintenance aid, though a few listeners never fully settle.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (n=39) has mean 6.74 (std 1.94) with meaningful confidence; distribution modestly right‑skewed. Qualitative responses (n=22) note moderate to significant improvement and high adviser usage (15 %). AutoGluon assigns the top low‑affect prediction (8.23), a substantial uplift over observed, suggesting high untapped potential. YouTube views: 8.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Mood lifts noticeably for many; adviser routing shows high usage, indicating strong appeal.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (n=25) yields mean 6.76 (std 1.71) with meaningful confidence; distribution similar to L1. Qualitative feedback (n=15) reports significant improvement and strong music love. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, again above observed. YouTube views: 8.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Good mood boost with many reporting significant improvement and loving the music.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (n=18) presents mean 6.28 (std 1.74) with developing confidence; distribution modestly left‑skewed. Qualitative data (n=25) notes noticeable improvement but also some ‘no effect’ reports. AutoGluon does not list this protocol among top low‑affect scores. YouTube views: 4.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Noticeable mood lift for most, but some report no effect.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (n=22) presents mean 6.36 (std 2.01) with meaningful confidence; distribution flat with many low scores, yielding pct_pos 45 %. Qualitative input (n=17) shows noticeable to significant improvement despite overall low binary rating. AutoGluon predicts 7.88 for motivation, indicating a large efficacy gap. YouTube views: 5.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation lifts for some, but overall many listeners give low scores despite noticing change.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (n=14) yields mean 6.21 (std 1.63) with developing confidence; distribution modestly centered and pct_pos 43 %. Qualitative feedback (n=21) includes strong music love but only slight to moderate improvement, suggesting a disconnect between affective response and motivational lift. AutoGluon predicts 7.88, again above observed. YouTube views: 7.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Motivation improves modestly; listeners love the music but report only slight gains.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (n=13) records mean 7.15 (std 2.38) with developing confidence; distribution is bimodal with high‑score tail, indicating a responder subgroup. Qualitative data (n=17) reveals moderate improvement overall but a non‑settle signal for some. AutoGluon predicts 7.88, close to observed high mean. YouTube views: 7. Drift similarity 0.507.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Motivation spikes for a subgroup, though others don’t feel much change.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern (n=24) yields mean 6.75 (std 1.62) with meaningful confidence; distribution modestly right‑skewed. Qualitative feedback (n=25) includes two ‘made things worse’ reports and strong music love. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for mind clarity, above observed, indicating room for improvement. YouTube views: 8.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mind clarity improves for most, though a few listeners feel worse after the session.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (n=16) shows mean 6.81 (std 1.64) with developing confidence; distribution is modestly centered. Qualitative data (n=20) reports significant improvement and very settling rhythm, with music loved by many. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a notable upward delta. YouTube views: 12.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Clearer thinking for many, with strong, soothing rhythm and liked music.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (n=10) records mean 5.70 (std 2.45) with developing confidence; distribution is highly bimodal and variance extreme. Qualitative comments (n=17) note a few ‘too busy’ rhythm critiques and two ‘worse’ outcomes. AutoGluon does not flag this protocol, underscoring a mismatch. YouTube views: 8. Drift similarity 0.696.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed results; some feel better while others report the rhythm as too busy and lower scores.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The library shows overall positive efficacy signals with HEALING and THRIVING near 7‑score and TRANSFORMING modestly lower, while variance and drift indicate evolving patterns.
• HEALING delivers the most consistent outcomes (mean 6.98, lowest std) across 12 protocols.
• THRIVING matches HEALING mean but shows a clear level‑gradient in Focus & Clarity protocols.
• TRANSFORMING exhibits the widest spread, with low‑affect zones under 6.0 and some high‑variance outliers.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
HEALING, THRIVING, and TRANSFORMING each display distinct efficacy signatures aligned with their design intents.
• HEALING DESCENT protocols cluster tightly around a 7.0 mean, with crisis entries modestly lower as expected.
• THRIVING HOLD protocols improve with longer levels, especially Focus & Clarity where L3 outperforms L1.
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols show the greatest variance, reflecting subgroup‑dependent responses.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols deviate noticeably from series trends or model expectations.
• X-MC-L3 (mean 5.70, std 2.45) falls below 6.0 and is highly bimodal, suggesting a population mismatch.
• T-FC-L3 (mean 7.63, std 1.97) records the highest THRIVING mean but exhibits strong cross‑run drift (0.532).
• H‑CR‑L1/L2/L3 share lower means (~6.4) yet qualitative ratings show unexpectedly high ‘Significant improvement’ counts.
• H‑OS‑L1 has the highest YouTube reach (30 views) but qualitative comments emphasize only ‘Noticeable improvement.’
• H‑SL‑L1 shows extreme analytical drift (0.530) despite strong outcome scores.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted protocol refinements are suggested by convergent evidence across analyses.
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] • X-MC-L3 (n=10) – redesign L3 descent curve or create a L3‑Lite variant; investigate volume‑specific groove effects.
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] • T-FC-L1 (n=41) – simplify rhythmic density in the hold phase to address ‘slightly busy’ feedback and raise mean scores.
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] • H-CR-L2 (n=33) – develop an ASCENT‑style crisis variant or cross‑bucket routing to X-ME-L1/L2, leveraging higher AutoGluon predictions.
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] • X-ME-L1 (n=39) – promote as primary low‑affect entry; increase adviser routing weight given top AutoGluon score (8.23).
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] • T-MB-L3 (n=14) – extend hold duration to better match phi‑sigmoid entrainment, addressing modest mean vs. predicted efficacy.
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] • X-MD-L3 (n=13) – perform population‑split analysis (caregiver vs. self) and create targeted routing rules for the high‑response subgroup.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals reveal pervasive analytical evolution and distinct protocol clusters.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 27 of 30 protocols show cosine similarity < 0.85; 10 below 0.65, indicating substantial framing shifts.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Tight clusters for T‑FC‑L1↔L2 (0.878) and H‑SL‑L1↔L2 (0.869); outliers include H‑OS‑L3, T‑FC‑L3, T‑CF‑L3.
• Confidence note: drift analysis spans ~2.5 months; cluster data derives from a single recent run, so interpretations should be hedged.

by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Crisis listeners report steady improvement; music opinion varies but outcomes are solid.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Quickly helps some in crisis, though a few dislike the music, leading to mixed feelings.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Great for overstimulation; people feel calmer and many love the music, though most note only noticeable change.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Helps calm overstimulated listeners with a very settling rhythm, though music reception is mixed.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Works well for overstimulation and even helps some fall asleep, with generally positive feedback.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Supports sleep onset quickly; listeners find the rhythm comforting and music enjoyable.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Provides steady sleep support, often used by caregivers; music is generally well received.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Helps many settle for sleep, though a few find the rhythm a bit busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Gentle boost to creative flow; many notice only slight improvement at first.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: More noticeable creative lift; listeners love the music and feel stronger improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Strong creative boost; most listeners report noticeable improvement and like the rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus improves but some find the rhythm a little busy, which may hold back the effect.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Better focus with moderate improvement; rhythm is still a bit busy for some listeners.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Excellent focus support; many report significant improvement and highly praise the experience.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Consistent maintenance support, especially in therapy settings; listeners report moderate improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Mixed maintenance results; some listeners note busy rhythms while others improve modestly.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Generally good maintenance aid, though a few listeners never fully settle.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mind clarity improves for most, though a few listeners feel worse after the session.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Clearer thinking for many, with strong, soothing rhythm and liked music.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Mixed results; some feel better while others report the rhythm as too busy and lower scores.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation lifts for some, but overall many listeners give low scores despite noticing change.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Motivation improves modestly; listeners love the music but report only slight gains.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Motivation spikes for a subgroup, though others don’t feel much change.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Mood lifts noticeably for many; adviser routing shows high usage, indicating strong appeal.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Good mood boost with many reporting significant improvement and loving the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Noticeable mood lift for most, but some report no effect.
